issue_title: Realm export contains rotated client secret
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

import-export

### Describe the bug

When creating a partial export of a realm the resulting json contains the "client.secret.rotated" attribute in "attributes" for an exported client (of course only when the feature 'client-secret-rotation' is enabled). This secret is not redacted/stripped.

### Version

20.0.3

### Expected behavior

I expected a redacted rotated client secret (`**********`), just like with the (non-rotated) client secret.

### Actual behavior

The unredacted rotated client secret is in the exported json

### How to Reproduce?

- start keycloak with `--features=client-secret-rotation`
- create a client profile with "executor": "secret-rotation"
- create a client policy
- client client and generate new secret
- partial export of realm setting, check the 'clients' checkbox


### Anything else?

I'll create a PR with the fix
commit_message:
Strip rotated client secret from export json (#19394)

Closes #19373

issue_title: memory leak when using ldap user federations
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

ldap

### Describe the bug

Hello,

We are running a keycloak cluster in production (using the embedded infinispan cache) using
version 18.0.2.

In 2023, we've experienced multiple outages due to the memory growing fast and crashing
1 node in 2-3 hours.

When these incidents started we had around 20 ldap user federations in our main realm
which were synchronized every 5m to 30m for "changed users sync" and
once a day for "full sync".  
We've also noticed that an internal tool was doing a lot of requests
(100-400 searches per minute) of an unknown user for minutes-hours compared to the normal load
(a few occasional searches throughout the day).

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/35383047/228295673-37ecb1d9-af43-4e6a-801e-7f149aa0b384.png)

We've analyzed a heap dump and found that 98% of the memory was retained by
the ConcurrentHashMap in `LDAPIdentityStoreRegistry.ldapStores`.  
The number of entries in it was correct compared to the number of ldap providers
we had so it wasn't due to the number of entries growing.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/35383047/228300173-ab2d1ab8-68b1-4be2-b3c2-df0a6787adc8.png)

In the meantime, we've also noticed something weird in our logs, we've seen a lot of
`[org.keycloak.storage.ldap.LDAPIdentityStoreRegistry] (Timer-0) Creating new LDAP Store for the LDAP storage provider`
logged the same second for a single provider.  
After analysis, these logs came from different threads and the only thing changing was the attribute `lastSync`.

Example:

- `thread-1 Creating new LDAP Store for the LDAP storage provider: provider1: lastSync=2`
- `thread-2 Creating new LDAP Store for the LDAP storage provider: provider1: lastSync=1`
- `thread-3 Creating new LDAP Store for the LDAP storage provider: provider1: lastSync=2`

The `LDAPIdentityStoreRegistry.ldapStores` cache is read (and updated ?) concurrently by multiple
threads when a synchronization happens.

In the example above, one request is updating the cache as the `lastSync`
field [changed](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/18.0.2/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/managers/UserStorageSyncManager.java#L149) during the synchronization, but another thread is updating it back to the older entry, etc...
We've seen this happening for 10 times in less than a second when the search load is high.

This race seems to happen in `LDAPIdentityStoreRegistry.getLdapStore` between the [line](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/18.0.2/federation/ldap/src/main/java/org/keycloak/storage/ldap/LDAPIdentityStoreRegistry.java#L41)
where the older context is retrieved from the cache and the check between it and the
config stored (earlier during the handling of a request) in `ldapModel`.

So it showed us that there were a lot of activity around this cache when a provider
is been synchronized. The more ldap synchronizations (wether due to a very low interval between sync or a lot of ldap providers) the more it is visible.  
As the number of entries was relevant in the cache itself, we suspected that older
entries couldn't be garbage collected as even by forcing a full gc
(e.g using `jmap -histo:live ...`), they were still in the heap.

During the heap analysis, we've noticed a lot of instances of `DefaultKeycloakSession`,
`LDAPIdentityStore`, `LDAPOperationManager` and many more of `LDAPStorageProvider`.

Immediate dominators of `DefaultKeycloakSession` was `LDAPIdentityStoreRegistry.ldapStores`
so it confirmed there was a problem with the garbage.

See also the dominator tree of one session:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/35383047/228301758-d04bc40c-5fab-4bf5-8db2-260db2668c28.png)

I can't explain it fully, but my theory is:

an `LDAPIdentityStore` needs a `DefaultKeycloakSession` at creation, each time a new one is created [here](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/18.0.2/federation/ldap/src/main/java/org/keycloak/storage/ldap/LDAPIdentityStoreRegistry.java#L56) before being put in the cache, the session passed is the current request session which is created when the request is [handled](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/18.0.2/quarkus/runtime/src/main/java/org/keycloak/quarkus/runtime/integration/web/QuarkusRequestFilter.java#L62) by the web server and is meant to be closed at its end.
It means this session won't be closed until it is removed from the cache.

Now when a request creates a `LDAPStorageProvider` (to talk to an ldap server) using the current
request session, it gets and obtain a reference to the latest `LDAPIdentityStore` from the cache
which uses another session (in our situation, we've seen dozens of LDAPStorageProvider
referencing a session stored in the `LDAPIdentityStoreRegistry`).

Sessions end up referenced by other sessions which seems to keep them to be garbage
collected (or something in their cache maybe).

I could reproduce this issue using the keycloak production server and the dev server on versions
`18.0.2`, `20.0.2` and `21.0.1`.

The simplest solution I could come up with was to create an `LDAPIdentityStore`
instance with a dedicated `DefaultKeycloakSession`.

```diff
--- a/federation/ldap/src/main/java/org/keycloak/storage/ldap/LDAPIdentityStoreRegistry.java
+++ b/federation/ldap/src/main/java/org/keycloak/storage/ldap/LDAPIdentityStoreRegistry.java
@@ -84,7 +84,8 @@ public class LDAPIdentityStoreRegistry {
         checkSystemProperty("com.sun.jndi.ldap.connect.pool.protocol", cfg.getConnectionPoolingProtocol(), "plain ssl");
         checkSystemProperty("com.sun.jndi.ldap.connect.pool.debug", cfg.getConnectionPoolingDebug(), "off");
 
-        return new LDAPIdentityStore(session, cfg);
+        KeycloakSession storeSession = session.getKeycloakSessionFactory().create();
+        return new LDAPIdentityStore(storeSession, cfg);
     }
```

We've tried it in our lab using scripts to reproduce the issue, by generating a search load
of 8000 searches / minute + 1 ldap synchronization every second and then in production.

We didn't notice any issues in 10 days, the memory is able to be garbage collected correctly.

A few note on this change:

- First I wanted to close the session explicitly when the context in the cache is changed
but as sessions were garbaged quickly I didn't do it, I don't know if there is an impact doing so.
- The session is used only by ldap related classes,  I didn't see any of them using
  a transaction to the db. If it does, the code above doesn't "close" the transaction
  (either by rollback or commit) like it is done [here](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/18.0.2/quarkus/runtime/src/main/java/org/keycloak/quarkus/runtime/integration/web/QuarkusRequestFilter.java#L121)


### Version

18.0.2, 20.0.2, 21.0.1

### Expected behavior

see above

### Actual behavior

see above

### How to Reproduce?

Here is a [project](https://github.com/ainquel/keycloak-memleak-ldap) to reproduce this issue. It uses `docker` and `docker-compose` (more instructions in the readme).

It creates a few ldap providers, starts lot of searches of an unknown user (to bypass the cache), synchronizes
providers constantly and monitors the heap and number of instances of a few classes. It allows to reproduce in a minute the behavior we've seen happening in hours / days.


### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Fix memory leak in LDAP

The caching in LDAP stores and reuses the session at the time of creating
`LDAPIdentityStore`. On top of that, there is not much cached, since apart
from the session which must not be part of long-lived cache, only config
is cached in the objects which is anyway always recomputed.

The cache for the LDAP still retains the LDAPConfig to keep
the `logLDAPConfig` call upon config change.

Closes: #19396

issue_title: Inconsistent use of Enum storage in legacy store
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

When investigating the H6 upgrade, I found that some entities are mapped to a TINYINT in the database, while others are mapped to VARCHAR(n), see https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/6cad9592f6464094543c39a85744080b0af4647e

On MySQL on Hibernate 6, this lead to problems, and was fixed by the annotation above. 

The "real" fix would be to use TINYINT consistently for all enums.

### Version

nightly

### Expected behavior

Hibernate mapping matches the mapping in the database. 

### Actual behavior

There is a discrepancy, which surfaced problems only on Hibernate 6 and MySQL

### How to Reproduce?

Run ResourceManagementTest

### Anything else?

As the Keycloak code doesn't contain any `@Enumerated(STRING)`, Keycloak saves all enums with the ordinal number. 

It should (?) be possible to change the type of the column at least for MySQL on the fly.
commit_message:
Consistent handling of enums in the database schema

Closes #19404

issue_title: Remove `em.refresh(realm)` during realm removal in `JpaRealmProvider`
issue_body:
There is https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/0a4456cc6169dc2ad1ecc8de6f862aafd00fa6f3/model/jpa/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/jpa/JpaRealmProvider.java#L169 in `JpaRealmProvider`. 

It seems it is redundant as there is the explicit lock there already at the start of the method. 

Relates to https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/19069

Original commit message says `concurrent transaction fix` and there is no issue for it: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/2315


Concurrent deletion and modification of realms is not well supported anyway, and might fail due to failing constraints, and would then be retried by the user.
commit_message:
Remove `em.refresh(realm)` call during realm removal in `JpaRealmProvider`

Closes #19430

issue_title: Remove access not working in new account v2 app
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

account/ui

### Describe the bug

This issue is a continuation of #12738. The new account v2 app cannot remove the consents for an app whose clientId needs to be encoded, for example, using a URL type clientId. The account v2 application fails with an error: `Not Found: RESTEASY003210: Could not find resource for full path: http://localhost:8080/realms/master/account/applications/http://localhost:8081/sample-rhsso-saml//consent`.

As you can see the URL is using the `clientId` without any encoding but the URL should be: `http://localhost:8080/admin/realms/master/users/85c9667d-1abe-4320-8008-62cfacd80519/consents/http%3A%2F%2Flocalhost%3A8081%2Fsample-rhsso-saml%2F`. Encoding the ID works at server side level.



### Version

21.0.1

### Expected behavior

The account v2 app should encode the `clientId` before sending the request.

### Actual behavior

The `clientId` is not encoded and the delete fails.

### How to Reproduce?

* Create a OIDC or SAML client with consent required to ON. Use as the client ID a URL type, in my case I created: `http://localhost:8081/sample-rhsso-saml/`.
* Login to the application and accept the consent page.
* Change to the account application: `http://localhost:8080/realms/master/account` Go to the `Applications` menu page. Expand the client with the URL and click `Remove Access`. The error appears.

### Anything else?

I have been checking and IMHO the only needed thing is just encoding the clientId. For example, in the [ApplicationsPage.tsx](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/21.0.2/themes/src/main/resources/theme/keycloak.v2/account/src/app/content/applications-page/ApplicationsPage.tsx#L102-L107) something like this:

```diff
   private removeConsent = (clientId: string) => {
-    this.context!.doDelete("/applications/" + clientId + "/consent")
+    this.context!.doDelete("/applications/" + encodeURIComponent(clientId) + "/consent")
       .then(() => {
         this.fetchApplications();
       });
```

But I a bit confused with the account app. Because I see it inside the `js` directory and inside the `themes` folder. So better if the UI team check this. But the summary is that the delete should send the `clientId` encoded.

issue_title: response_type none is oidc spec but ignored in the current implementation.
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

If a request to the token endpoint is including the `response_type none` param, it is ignored and a token response is sent.




### Version

20.0.5

### Expected behavior

From the open id connect specs (https://openid.net/specs/oauth-v2-multiple-response-types-1_0.html)

### None Response Type
This section registers the Response Type none, in accordance with the stipulations in the OAuth 2.0 specification, Section 8.4. The intended purpose is to enable use cases where a party requests the Authorization Server to register a grant of access to a Protected Resource on behalf of a Client but requires no access credentials to be returned to the Client at that time. The means by which the Client eventually obtains the access credentials is left unspecified here.

One scenario is where a user wishes to purchase an application from a market, and desires to authorize application installation and grant the application access to Protected Resources in a single step. However, since the user is not presently interacting with the (not yet active) application, it is not appropriate to return access credentials simultaneously in the authorization step.

When supplied as the response_type parameter in an OAuth 2.0 Authorization Request, the Authorization Server SHOULD NOT return an OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code, Access Token, Access Token Type, or ID Token in a successful response to the grant request. If a redirect_uri is supplied, the User Agent SHOULD be redirected there after granting or denying access. The request MAY include a state parameter, and if so, the Authorization Server MUST echo its value as a response parameter when issuing either a successful response or an error response. The default Response Mode for this Response Type is the query encoding. Both successful and error responses SHOULD be returned using the supplied Response Mode, or if none is supplied, using the default Response Mode.
The Response Type none SHOULD NOT be combined with other Response Types.



### Actual behavior

`response_type none` is ignored and a token is returned in the response. 

### How to Reproduce?

NOTE: This section is not correct. See this comment for the details https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/19564#issuecomment-2095850539

```
POST /auth/realms/<realm-name>/protocol/openid-connect/token HTTP/1.1
Host: <host>
Authorization: Basic XXX
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded

grant_type=password&client_id=<client-id>&password=<password>&
username=<username>&grant_type=password&response_type=none
```

### Anything else?

https://openid.net/specs/oauth-v2-multiple-response-types-1_0.html
commit_message:
OIDC dynamic client registration with response_type=none

Closes #19564

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Graziano <g.graziano94@gmail.com>

issue_title: Maven 3.9 removes plexus-utils
issue_body: ### Description According to the [release notes for Maven 3.9](https://maven.apache.org/docs/3.9.0/release-notes.html#potentially-breaking-core-changes-if-migrating-from-3-8-x): > Maven 2.x was auto-injecting an ancient version of plexus-utils dependency into the plugin classpath, and Maven 3.x continued doing this to preserve backward compatibility. Starting with Maven 3.9, it does not happen anymore. This change may lead to plugin breakage. The fix for affected plugin maintainers is to explicitly declare a dependency on plexus-utils. The workaround for affected plugin users is to add this dependency to plugin dependencies until issue is fixed by the affected plugin maintainer. See [MNG-6965](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/MNG-6965). This results in a build error when using a newer version of Maven. The solution is to make the following change to the main `pom.xml`: ```xml com.samaxes.maven minify-maven-plugin ${minify.plugin.version} org.codehaus.plexus plexus-utils 3.2.1 ``` The `minify-maven-plugin` does have [a PR to fix this](https://github.com/samaxes/minify-maven-plugin/pull/176), but it's been open since February, and no commits have been made to the repo since 2016.
commit_message: Remove unused `minify-maven-plugin` dependency (#19583)

issue_title: Custom User Storage Provider doesn't look up users after saving changes
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

When using a custom user provider and configuring it, everything works as expected.
I am able to log in with users from this federation and I can see them in the 'Users' tab.

After trying to edit the provider (User federation -> Provider details) and saving changes, the provider no longer works as expected. (It is enough to 'fake' the changes - delete a single letter and revert - then save)

- I can still see the logs from the UserStorageProviderFactory `getConfigProperties()` and `validateConfiguration()` methods implementations
- I can no longer see the logs from the factory `create()` method (which was visible and invoked before the changes)

This problem started occurring after upgrading from 18 to 21

Current workaround:

- delete the provider and configure it again
- disable and re-enable the provider (yes, this is enough for it to start working again)

I tried running KC with debug logs but can't see anything meaningful.
I do not see any problem with the plugin implementation (I was trying to check with the reference implementation: https://github.com/dasniko/keycloak-extensions-demo/tree/main/peanuts-userprovider)

### Version

21.0.1

### Expected behavior

Custom user provider still works as expected after saving changes.

### Actual behavior

The provider is fetching its configuration but is not invoked for searching the users.

### How to Reproduce?

- Keycloak extension for custom user provider
- configure a working example
- make changes to the configuration (or just fake changes) and save
- the keycloak doesn't invoke the implementation of the storage provider's `UserStorageProviderFactory` `create()` method
- after disabling and enabling the provider, everything should work again

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Members are inhereted from LDAP group with the same name
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

I have such groups:
* global_ldap
    * office 
 * global_local
     * office  

Members, synced from LDAP, of the group `/global_ldap/office` also appear as members of the local Keycloak DB group `/global_local/office`

### Version

21.0.2

### Expected behavior

Members of those two groups, with unique paths, but the same names. should be unique.
E.g.:
Groups:
* "global_ldap"
    * "office", Members: John, George
 * "global_local"
     * "office", Members: < none >

### Actual behavior

Groups:
* "global_ldap"
    * "office", Members: John, George
 * "global_local"
     * "office", Members: John, George

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create a mapper for LDAP group, import group - for example, `/global_ldap/office`
2. Create new Keycloak local group in some different group path, that is also called `office` - for example `/global_local/office`
3. Check members of the local group `/global_local/office`, normally there should be none, but you will see members of LDAP group `/global_ldap/office`

### Anything else?

It only happens when LDAP group is involved, local (keycloak's) groups with the same names, but different paths, do not have this issue.

issue_title: Group list isn't filtered based on permission like user lists
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

account/api

### Describe the bug

In Fine Grain Admin Permissions, we would like to grant permission on specific users in the specific group. The bug is that all groups have been retrieved even though some groups don't have the access right to view based on fine grain permission. In users, we don't have that issues. 

### Version

version 21.0.1

### Expected behavior

only retrieved the fine grained access group in the list. 

### Actual behavior

all groups have been retrieved 

### How to Reproduce?

How to Reproduce?
login to the admin console with the admin account

Create the new realm and give proper name as (Trial)

Switch to the Trial (realm) from the master realm in the master

create the Trial_admin user
4_1) Switch to the user menu, create the Trial_admin (user).
4_2) To the Trial_admin user, add the credential with your password.
4_3) To the Trial_admin user, add the realm-admin role. How to add the role
a) go to the Role mapping in the Users Menu
b) click on Assign role button, Assign roles pop up will appear
c) go to the Filter by realm roles, click on Filter by clients
d) search realm-admin and then add it

create group1_adminuser
5_1) click add user button to add group1_admin (user).
5_2) To the group1_admin user, add the credential with your password.
5_3) To the group1_admin user, add the query-clients, query-users and query-group roles. How to add the role
a) go to the Role mapping in the Users Menu
b) click on Assign role button, Assign roles pop up will appear
c) go to the Filter by realm roles, click on Filter by clients
d) search query-clients, query-users and query-group and then add it

create group2_adminuser
5_1) click add user button to add group2_admin (user).
5_2) To the group2_admin user, add the credential with your password.
5_3) To the group2_admin user, add the query-clients, query-users and query-group roles. How to add the role
a) go to the Role mapping in the Users Menu
b) click on Assign role button, Assign roles pop up will appear
c) go to the Filter by realm roles, click on Filter by clients
d) search query-clients, query-users and query-group and then add it

create group 1 under Groups menu
7_1) add the group1_admin into group 1

create group 2 under Groups menu
8_1)add the group2_admin into group 1

enable the fine grain permission in any group (permission enable)

To create the policy for group
10_1) go to the Clients menu
10_2) click on real-management and then go to the Authorization tab
10_3) go to the Policies tab under Authorization tab
10_4) Click on Create policy button to add group1_admin_policy
10_5) select Group in the pop up for policy type
10_6) in the group policy pop up, give the name (group1_admin_policy)
10_7) join the group to the group1 and then save
10_8) Click on the create policy button to add group2_admin_policy
10_9) repeat the step for group 2 admin policy from 10_5 to 10_7

apply those policy in the group 1 and group 2 in the permission tab, go to permission and select manage_members and view permission and then add the proper permission

logout form the admin console and switch to the Trial Realm

then login with group1_admin to the Trial Realm

by right, group1_admin should only see the group 1 in the group and should not see group 2. 

### Anything else?

_No response_

### Area

issue_title: Refresh token have a negative exp claim because TokenManager is vulnerable to integer overflow for long lasting sessions (YEAR 2038 bug)
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

Refresh tokens (access tokens probably also impacted) are generated with a negative expiry timestamp when the SSO session max length is set to a big value (several years).

The bug is located here in TokenManager class.

            int sessionExpires = userSession.getStarted()
                + (userSession.isRememberMe() && realm.getSsoSessionMaxLifespanRememberMe() > 0
                    ? realm.getSsoSessionMaxLifespanRememberMe()
                    : realm.getSsoSessionMaxLifespan());
            expiration = expiration <= sessionExpires ? expiration : sessionExpires;

This is known as year 2038 issue. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Year_2038_problem

### Version

21.0.2

### Expected behavior

The expiry of the token is properly set.

### Actual behavior

The expiry of the token is set in the past.

### How to Reproduce?

Set a very long value for SSO max session and get a refresh token, for example 5400 days.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Gzip cache is only invalidated upon Keycloak version changes
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

Keycloak encodes theme resources with gzip and caches them in the `/keycloak/data/kc-gzip-cache/<resourcesVersion>` folder. Unfortunately the `resourcesVersion` part of the folder name seems to only depend on the Keycloak version as set in `/keycloak/lib/lib/main/org.keycloak.keycloak-common-21.0.2.jar::keycloak-version.properties`.

### Version

21.0.2

### Expected behavior

The `<resourcesVersion>` folder name should also depend on, for example, a user-configurable value so that it's possible to replace a provider jar that contains resources (e.g. CSS files for a theme), without necessarily updating Keycloak.

### Actual behavior

The `<resourcesVersion>` only d epends on the Keycloak version. Therefore the gzip cache is not invalidated if a provider jar is changed and an outdated resource is returned.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create a custom theme as part of a provider, which could look like this:
```
.
├── pom.xml
└── src
    └── main
        └── resources
            ├── META-INF
            │   └── keycloak-themes.json
            └── theme
                └── example
                    └── login
                        ├── resources
                        │   └── main.css
                        └── theme.properties
```
2. Build the provider with `mvn install`
3. Copy the jar file to `/keycloak/providers`
4. Restart Keycloak
5. Change e.g. `main.css`, rebuild, copy and restart (repeat steps 2-4)
6. Observe that `main.css.gz` in `/keycloak/data/tmp/kc-gzip-cache/<resourcesVersion>` does not reflect the change from step 5

### Anything else?

As mentioned in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/12060#issuecomment-1134606711, disabling the gzip provider circumvents the problem that outdated files are served. However, this still leaves us with browser cache busting problem (default max age value for theme resources is 30 days).
commit_message:
Docs note about manually delete themes cache
closes #19675

issue_title: AlreadyLoggedIn when impersonating a user in a SAML client
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

Checking the issue #15337 I noticed that in the current main branch impersonation does not work when using a SAML client. I think that the admin user should be in the same realm than the impersonated user too. After some debugging the problem is that the AUTH_SESSION_ID is not returned for the new impersonated session and this creates issues.

The bug is intoduced in 20.0.2 and I think that probably triggered by this commit https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/15981/commits/a1ab33778df468d5b9c1828918eedfe7faca1722. 

I'm going to send a little PR that creates the root authentication session and assigns the AUTH_SESSION_ID cookie when doing an impersonation. This way everything seems to to work.

### Version

21.0.2

### Expected behavior

The SAML application should work with the impersonation.

### Actual behavior

Message `You are already logged in` is displayed when going to keycloak login page to authenticate.

### How to Reproduce?

Install a SAML client (in my case is a EAP 7.4 with SAML adapters installed). And do the following:

* Login in the realm console with an admin user that can impersonate. The admin should be in the same realm (master in my case).
* Go to users an impersonate another user.
* The accuount console works.
* Go to the SAML application.
* It redirects to the login page and the error `You are already logged in` is displayed.

As commented I think the reason is commit https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/6086201fe0472b89eb3d4e188bd4132c29f8d4c9, which checked AUTH_SESSION_ID cookie.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: SAML Encryption: Missing Support for http://www.w3.org/2009/xmlenc11#rsa-oaep
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

saml

### Describe the bug

Since Keycloak 21 it is no longer possible to decrypt SAML Assertions encrypted with Algorithm `http://www.w3.org/2009/xmlenc11#rsa-oaep` using MGF1 with SHA256. Only `http://www.w3.org/2001/04/xmlenc#rsa-oaep-mgf1p` is supported. This was previously discussed here https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/15985#issuecomment-1359756472. However the XMLEnc specification referenced there is outdated and misses a key detail: RSA-OAEP can both be declared using  `http://www.w3.org/2001/04/xmlenc#rsa-oaep-mgf1p` (meaning RSA OAEP with MGF1 with SHA1) AND `http://www.w3.org/2009/xmlenc11#rsa-oaep`. The latter MAY be combined with a MGF declaration, allowing for instance RSA OAEP with MGF1 with SHA256.

We are using an IdP that encrypts SAML Assertions like this:

```xml
     <xenc:EncryptionMethod Algorithm="http://www.w3.org/2009/xmlenc11#rsa-oaep">
      <ds:DigestMethod Algorithm="http://www.w3.org/2001/04/xmlenc#sha256"></ds:DigestMethod>
      <xenc11:MGF xmlns:xenc11="http://www.w3.org/2009/xmlenc11#" Algorithm="http://www.w3.org/2009/xmlenc11#mgf1sha256"></xenc11:MGF>
     </xenc:EncryptionMethod>
```

This appears to be perfectly valid according to the current XMLEnc spec: https://www.w3.org/TR/xmlenc-core1/#sec-RSA-OAEP

Up until Keycloak 20 this was supported (kind of at least, seeing as encryption and signature keys were mixed), but the changes introduced through https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/15985 limit Keycloak to just http://www.w3.org/2001/04/xmlenc#rsa-oaep-mgf1p and http://www.w3.org/2001/04/xmlenc#rsa-1_5. 

This breaks existing functionality and means we cannot update to Keycloak 21.





### Version

21.0.2

### Expected behavior

http://www.w3.org/2009/xmlenc11#rsa-oaep as SAML encryption algorithm is supported, including declaring the MGF to be used as defined in https://www.w3.org/TR/xmlenc-core1/#sec-RSA-OAEP

### Actual behavior

http://www.w3.org/2009/xmlenc11#rsa-oaep is not supported for SAML encryption

### How to Reproduce?

Trying to decrypt a SAML Assertion encrypted like this

```xml
     <xenc:EncryptionMethod Algorithm="http://www.w3.org/2009/xmlenc11#rsa-oaep">
      <ds:DigestMethod Algorithm="http://www.w3.org/2001/04/xmlenc#sha256"></ds:DigestMethod>
      <xenc11:MGF xmlns:xenc11="http://www.w3.org/2009/xmlenc11#" Algorithm="http://www.w3.org/2009/xmlenc11#mgf1sha256"></xenc11:MGF>
     </xenc:EncryptionMethod>
```

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Adding support for rsa-oaep for SAML encryption
Closes https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/19689

issue_title: Operator restarts occasionally result in recreation of managed keycloak Statefulset Pods
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

operator

### Describe the bug

When the operator container is restarted occasionally the pod template of the keycloak statefulsets managed by the operator changes which leads to a recreation of the keycloak containers managed by the operator.

I noticed the order of environment variables in the pod template changed, which I think is the reason for the recreation.

Pre operator container restart keycloak statefulset spec:
```yaml
spec:
  ...
  template:
    ...
    spec:
      containers:
      - args:
        - start
        env:
        - name: KC_CACHE_STACK
          value: kubernetes
        - name: KC_CACHE
          value: ispn
        - name: KC_HEALTH_ENABLED
          value: "true"
        - name: KC_PROXY
          value: edge
      ...
```

Post operator container restart keycloak statefulset spec:
```yaml
spec:
  ...
  template:
    ...
    spec:
      containers:
      - args:
        - start
        env:
        - name: KC_CACHE_STACK
          value: kubernetes
        - name: KC_HEALTH_ENABLED
          value: "true"
        - name: KC_CACHE
          value: ispn
        - name: KC_PROXY
          value: edge
      ...
```

### Version

20.0.5

### Expected behavior

Even though the keycloak operator container was restarted, reconciliation should not result in a recreation of the managed keycloak statefulset pods if no configuration was changed.

### Actual behavior

Keycloak Pods are recreated by the operator when it restarts.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Install the keycloak operator in a kubernetes cluster
2. Create a valid Keycloak Resource without specifying the [default configurations](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/20.0.5/operator/src/main/java/org/keycloak/operator/Constants.java#L42-L47)
5. Wait until the keycloak StatefulSet is ready and reconciliation of the Keycloak Resource has successfully finished
6. Redeploy the keycloak operator multiple times and notice the order of the environment variables in the Keycloak Statefulset's pod template to change and that keycloak pods are being recreated



### Anything else?

I think the reason for the behavior is that a Map is used for the 
[default configurations](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/20.0.5/operator/src/main/java/org/keycloak/operator/Constants.java#L42-L47) 
which does not guarantee order when converted later to a list.
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/20.0.5/operator/src/main/java/org/keycloak/operator/controllers/KeycloakDeployment.java#L434-L436
commit_message: Stops the recreation of managed Keycloak Statefulset Pods when Keycloak Operator restarts occasionally. (#20187)

issue_title: JPA Map storage not fully initialized when DB not available at start
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

When the DB is not available, on Quarkus the EMF is created, but `JpaMapUtils.addSpecificNamedQueries` fails. Due to that, the JMSPF is half-initialized and the named queries are missing. 

JPA map storage is using lazy initialization, so it is initialized on first access wrapped in a global lock which will retry. 

### Version

nightly

### Expected behavior

EMF should be fully initialized

### Actual behavior

If the database is not available when initializing the EMF  *and* then becomes available while waiting for the lock, *and* CockroachDB is used, then on the first login there is the following error message in the log and login fails: 

> No query defined for that name [deleteRootAuthenticationSessionByIdNoOptimisticLocking]

### How to Reproduce?

* Stop CockroachDB
* Start Keycloak Quarkus, wait for first error message to appear
* Start CockroachDB
* See startup of Keycloak complete
* Log in 

### Anything else?

I will create a PR.

Seen when testing on keycloak-benchmark continuous integration runs where Keycloak already starts while DB is not yet available.
commit_message:
Fix half-initialized EMF for JPA Map Storage

Closes #19741

issue_title: Group Membership nested token claimnot working
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

When using the group membership mapper to LDAP groups to a claim that needs to be nested, the claim on the JWT will be actually a string and not a nested object.

### Version

21.0.2

### Expected behavior

Mapper Type: Group Membership
Name: groups
Token Claim Name: https://hasura\.io/jwt/claims.x-hasura-groups

```json
"https://hasura.io/jwt/claims": {
    "x-hasura-groups": [
      "JIRA_User",
      "CONFLUENCE_User",
      "BITBUCKET_USER"
    ],
}
```

### Actual behavior

Mapper Type: Group Membership
Name: groups
Token Claim Name: https://hasura\.io/jwt/claims.x-hasura-groups

```json
"https://hasura\\.io/jwt/claims.x-hasura-groups": [
    "JIRA_User",
    "CONFLUENCE_User",
    "BITBUCKET_USER"
  ],
```

### How to Reproduce?

- Connect to LDAP or create some groups on Keycloak.
- Configure the mapper:

Mapper Type: Group Membership
Name: groups
Token Claim Name: https://hasura\.io/jwt/claims.x-hasura-groups

- Enable the claims to be sent on the JWT
- Retrieve a JWT by logging in
- Decode the JWT

### Anything else?

I tried to not escape the first dot, and the claims is again sent back as a sigle string. Seems like the mapper never creates a nested object.

```json
  "https://hasura.io/jwt/claims.x-hasura-groups": [
    "JIRA_User",
    "CONFLUENCE_User",
    "BITBUCKET_USER"
  ],
```

This is a critical problem for our project as Hasura can only read claims inside `https://hasura.io/jwt/claims`.
The nested objects work with every other mapper, it is just the Groups Membership that is breaking.
commit_message:
Use OIDCAttributeMapperHelper.mapClaim in the GroupMembershipMapper
Closes https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/19767
